SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The paper states its main claims clearly (Sections 1–3) and supports them with extensive experiments across four scenarios in Doom (Section 4, Tables 1–4) including ablations and generalization tests. The modelling and training procedure are well specified (ot = <st,mt>, definition of f and goal u(f;g) in Eq. 1, predictor F in Eq. 2–4, action selection in Eq. 3) and the experiments compare to reasonable baselines (DQN, A3C, DSR) and include multiple runs. Limitations include reliance on the assumption that goals can be expressed as functions of future measurements (Eq. 1), no formal convergence proofs for the learning scheme, and relatively limited statistical reporting (mostly averages over 3 runs and no significance tests); these are minor and do not undermine the empirical conclusions for the evaluated domains.

ORIGINALITY.justification: The paper presents a distinct formulation that reduces control to supervised prediction of future measurement vectors conditioned on goal parameters (Eqs. 1–3) and a practical architecture (perception/measurement/goal modules and expectation/action streams in Eq. 6–7). While it builds on prior ideas (e.g. successor representations, Monte Carlo prediction, and the dueling/expectation/action split cited from Schaul et al. (2015) and Wang et al. (2016)), the combination — goal-parameterized multi-horizon vector prediction applied to rich visual control and demonstrated empirically — is a non-trivial and notable contribution rather than a mere rehash of existing methods.

CLARITY.justification: The paper is well organized (Intro → Background → Model → Experiments → Discussion) and introduces notation consistently (ot = <st,mt>, f, g, F(·) in Section 3) with key equations referenced by number. The architecture and training procedures are described clearly and experimental scenarios and metrics are spelled out (Section 4, scenario descriptions and offsets τ1…τn). Minor presentation issues are that some implementation/hyperparameter details are deferred to appendices and figures/tables are referenced but not present here, but overall the exposition is clear and readable.
